memory.summary: I could easily slip into leisure now, but that project is due Thursday and barely started. My relationships matter most, yet letting this slide would hurt my growth and stress me out later.

desires: Ensure I don't let my close friends down by missing our dinner plans tomorrow, even if the project is still vague.
Push through the immediate urge to slack off on the term project so I don't face a crisis Thursday that ruins my reputation.
Avoid the guilt of wasting today's energy on trivial chores when I should be building momentum for the future payoff.

goals.description: Block 2 hours of deep work on the term project to build momentum before Thursday's deadline, resisting the urge to slack off.
goals.rationale: My long planning horizon and risk tolerance of 3 mean I cannot afford a crisis that ruins my reputation. The 'soft' hardness of the project is a trap; I need deliberate effort now to secure future payoff.

goals.description: Resist doing the trivial chore or attending leisure immediately; schedule those for later once project momentum is established.
goals.rationale: While affiliation with cohort_3 is my top value (0.5), missing the dinner is only a risk if I don't act *now* on the project first. Doing the quick chore wastes the high energy I have right now for something that yields no future growth. I will protect my focus capacity by deferring these distractions.

agent.last_reasoning: I must lock in momentum on the term project now; its 'soft' difficulty is a trap. Deferring leisure protects my focus, but I'll text cohort_3 soon to reaffirm our bond without breaking my deep work streak.
